name: Kalsubai Peak
tagline: Stand on the rooftop of Maharashtra.
short: The highest peak in Maharashtra. A night-trek classic with iron ladders, monsoon clouds and a sunrise that spills over the entire Sahyadri range.
long: Known as the 'Everest of Maharashtra', Kalsubai rises to 1,646m above sea level. The trail climbs through paddy terraces and forest before a series of fixed iron ladders take you up the final rock faces. Time it for the monsoon and you'll walk inside the clouds; time it for winter and you'll catch a sea of fog at sunrise with Alang, Madan and Kulang floating above it.

itinerary.title: Assemble at Bari Village
itinerary.detail: Meet your trek leader, gear check and a quick briefing before the ascent.

itinerary.title: Summit push
itinerary.detail: Climb the ladder sections under headlamps to reach the peak before sunrise.

itinerary.title: Sunrise & descent
itinerary.detail: Soak in the views, visit the summit temple, then descend for breakfast.

name: Harishchandragad Fort
tagline: Sleep beside the legendary Konkan Kada.
short: An ancient hill-fort wrapped in caves, temples and the jaw-dropping concave cliff of Konkan Kada — famous for its circular rainbow halos.
long: Harishchandragad is a trek into history. The plateau holds the 6th-century Harishchandreshwar temple, the Kedareshwar cave with its waist-deep ice-cold water, and the surreal Konkan Kada — a 300m overhanging cliff that occasionally produces a rare circular rainbow. We camp on the plateau under a blanket of stars before exploring the caves at dawn.

itinerary.title: Khireshwar to plateau
itinerary.detail: Trek up via Tolar Khind, explore the temple complex and set up camp.

itinerary.title: Konkan Kada sunset
itinerary.detail: Walk to the cliff edge for one of the Sahyadris' finest sunsets.

itinerary.title: Caves & descent
itinerary.detail: Sunrise, visit Kedareshwar cave, then a steady descent back to base.

name: Rajmachi Twin Forts
tagline: Fireflies, forts and a Sahyadri night walk.
short: A gentle, beginner-friendly trail to the twin forts of Shrivardhan and Manaranjan — magical in monsoon and famous for its firefly season.
long: Rajmachi is the perfect first trek. The well-trodden trail from Lonavala winds through Udhewadi village to the twin forts of Shrivardhan and Manaranjan. In the early monsoon, the surrounding forests light up with thousands of fireflies, and waterfalls appear around every bend. It's an easy grade with a big reward.

itinerary.title: Lonavala trailhead
itinerary.detail: Briefing and an easy forest walk towards Udhewadi village.

itinerary.title: Twin forts
itinerary.detail: Explore Shrivardhan & Manaranjan with valley views all around.

itinerary.title: Return
itinerary.detail: Descend at golden hour, with firefly spotting in season.

name: Kedarkantha Summit
tagline: India's most loved winter snow trek.
short: A picture-perfect Himalayan winter summit through snow-laden pine forests, frozen clearings and a sunrise ridge at 3,810m.
long: Kedarkantha is the trek that turns first-timers into mountain lovers. Starting from the village of Sankri, the trail passes the storybook clearings of Juda-ka-Talab and Kedarkantha base, camping on fresh snow each night. The pre-dawn summit climb rewards you with a 360° panorama of Swargarohini, Bandarpoonch and Black Peak glowing in the first light.

itinerary.title: Drive to Sankri
itinerary.detail: Scenic transfer from Dehradun to the base village (1,920m).

itinerary.title: Sankri to Juda-ka-Talab
itinerary.detail: Forest trail to the frozen lake clearing (2,700m).

itinerary.title: To Kedarkantha Base
itinerary.detail: Climb to the open meadow base camp (3,500m).

itinerary.title: Summit & descend
itinerary.detail: Pre-dawn push to the summit (3,810m), descend to Hargaon.

itinerary.title: Hargaon to Sankri
itinerary.detail: Final forest descent back to the village.

itinerary.title: Return to Dehradun
itinerary.detail: Morning drive back with a lifetime of memories.

name: Hampta Pass Crossing
tagline: Cross from green valleys into a cold desert.
short: A dramatic crossover trek — lush Kullu valleys on one side, the stark moonscape of Lahaul on the other, linked by a 4,270m pass.
long: Hampta Pass is a trek of contrasts. You begin in the green, river-fed meadows of the Kullu valley and climb to the narrow notch of the pass at 4,270m. Step over the other side and the landscape flips entirely into the barren, high-altitude desert of Lahaul. The trip ends with a side-trip to the surreal blue waters of Chandratal lake.
highlights: Lush Kullu meadows to barren Lahaul

itinerary.title: Manali to Jobra to Chika
itinerary.detail: Short drive then an easy walk to the first riverside camp.

itinerary.title: Chika to Balu-ka-Ghera
itinerary.detail: Follow the Rani river through meadows to base.

itinerary.title: Pass crossing to Shea Goru
itinerary.detail: Summit the pass (4,270m) and descend into Lahaul.

itinerary.title: Shea Goru to Chatru & Chandratal
itinerary.detail: Descend, then drive to the alpine lake.

itinerary.title: Return to Manali
itinerary.detail: Drive back over Rohtang with the Himalayas at your back.

name: Valley of Flowers
tagline: A UNESCO meadow that blooms once a year.
short: A UNESCO World Heritage valley carpeted with hundreds of alpine flower species, paired with a pilgrimage to Hemkund Sahib.
long: For a few weeks each monsoon, the Valley of Flowers erupts into a riot of colour — over 300 species of alpine blooms set against glacier-fed streams and snow-tipped peaks. This gentle trek pairs the UNESCO-listed valley with a steep but rewarding climb to the sacred glacial lake of Hemkund Sahib at 4,300m.

itinerary.title: Rishikesh to Govindghat
itinerary.detail: Scenic drive along the Alaknanda river.

itinerary.title: Govindghat to Ghangaria
itinerary.detail: Trek up to the base village for the valley.

itinerary.title: Valley of Flowers
itinerary.detail: A full day exploring the blooming valley.

itinerary.title: Hemkund Sahib
itinerary.detail: Climb to the sacred high-altitude lake (4,300m).

itinerary.title: Descend to Govindghat
itinerary.detail: Return trek to the road head.

itinerary.title: Drive to Rishikesh
itinerary.detail: Final transfer back to the plains.
